audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Judges / Shoftim Gideon's pursuit, revenge, kingship refusal, ephod, and aftermath narrative, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Ephraim conflict de-escalation, Succoth/Penuel refusal and punishment, Zebah and Zalmunna, revenge for brothers, kingship offer/refusal, gold collection, ephod snare, many wives and sons, Abimelech, and post-Gideon relapse.

verses.restored: Men of Ephraim said to him, What is this thing you did to us, not calling us when you went to fight Midian? They quarreled with him strongly.
He said to them, What have I done now compared with you? Are not gleanings of Ephraim better than vintage of Abiezer?
Into your hand Divine gave princes of Midian, Oreb and Zeeb; what could I do compared with you? Then their spirit relaxed from against him when he spoke this word.
Gideon came to Jordan, crossing over, he and three hundred men with him, weary yet pursuing.
He said to men of Succoth, Please give loaves of bread to people at my feet, for they are weary, and I am pursuing after Zebah and Zalmunna, kings of Midian.
Officials of Succoth said, Is palm of Zebah and Zalmunna already in your hand, that we should give bread to your army?
Gideon said, Therefore when YHWH gives Zebah and Zalmunna into my hand, I will thresh your flesh with thorns of wilderness and with briers.
He went up from there to Penuel and spoke to them similarly, and men of Penuel answered him as men of Succoth answered.
He said also to men of Penuel, When I return in peace, I will tear down this tower.
Zebah and Zalmunna were in Karkor, and their camps with them, about fifteen thousand, all remaining from entire camp of sons of east; fallen were one hundred twenty thousand men drawing sword.
Gideon went up by way of tent-dwellers east of Nobah and Jogbehah and struck camp while camp was secure.
Zebah and Zalmunna fled, and he pursued after them, captured two kings of Midian, Zebah and Zalmunna, and terrified all camp.
Gideon son of Joash returned from battle by ascent of Heres.
He captured a young man from men of Succoth and questioned him, and he wrote down for him officials of Succoth and its elders, seventy-seven men.
He came to men of Succoth and said, Look, Zebah and Zalmunna, about whom you taunted me, saying, Is palm of Zebah and Zalmunna already in your hand, that we should give bread to your weary men?
He took elders of city and thorns of wilderness and briers, and with them he made men of Succoth know.
Tower of Penuel he tore down, and he killed men of city.
He said to Zebah and Zalmunna, What kind of men were they whom you killed at Tabor? They said, As you are, so were they; each one like form of sons of king.
He said, My brothers, sons of my mother, they were. As YHWH lives, if you had preserved them alive, I would not kill you.
He said to Jether his firstborn, Rise, kill them. But the young man did not draw his sword, for he feared, because he was still a youth.
Zebah and Zalmunna said, Rise yourself and fall upon us, for as the man is, so is his strength. Gideon arose and killed Zebah and Zalmunna, and took crescent ornaments on necks of their camels.
Men of Israel said to Gideon, Rule over us, both you and your son and your son's son, for you saved us from hand of Midian.
Gideon said to them, I will not rule over you, and my son shall not rule over you; YHWH shall rule over you.
Gideon said to them, Let me ask a request from you: each man give me earring from his spoil. For they had gold earrings, because they were Ishmaelites.
They said, We will surely give. They spread garment, and each man threw there an earring from his spoil.
Weight of gold earrings he requested was one thousand seven hundred gold shekels, besides crescents, pendants, purple garments on kings of Midian, and chains on necks of their camels.
Gideon made it into an ephod and placed it in his city, Ophrah; all Israel prostituted themselves after it there, and it became snare to Gideon and to his house.
Midian was subdued before children of Israel and did not lift its head again; land was quiet forty years in days of Gideon.
Jerubbaal son of Joash went and dwelt in his house.
Gideon had seventy sons going out from his thigh, for he had many wives.
His concubine who was in Shechem also bore him a son, and he placed his name Abimelech.
Gideon son of Joash died in good old age and was buried in tomb of Joash his father, in Ophrah of Abiezrites.
It was, when Gideon died, that children of Israel returned and prostituted themselves after the Baals and made Baal-berith their god.
Children of Israel did not remember YHWH their Divine, who had rescued them from hand of all their enemies around,
and they did not do covenant loyalty with house of Jerubbaal-Gideon according to all good he had done with Israel.

translation_notes: Gideon de-escalates Ephraim's anger with humility and honor-sharing, a constructive contrast to his later vengeance against Succoth and Penuel.
Succoth and Penuel refuse aid out of political risk while Gideon's men are weary; Gideon's response becomes torture, tower destruction, and killing. This is revenge, not justice.
The young man from Succoth is compelled into identifying seventy-seven elders/officials; the coercive information-gathering context must not be ignored.
Gideon tries to make his son Jether execute captured kings, but the youth is afraid and does not do it. Children should not be trained into killing to prove maturity.

Gideon verbally refuses dynastic rule and says YHWH will rule, but his wealth, many wives, seventy sons, concubine, and son named Abimelech ('my father is king') expose a practical drift toward kingship.
The gold ephod becomes a religious snare to Gideon and his house; even a victorious leader's memorial object can become spiritually dangerous.
The return to Baal-berith after Gideon's death continues the book's pattern of fragile memory and post-leader relapse.
safety_note: Do not use Judges 8 to justify torture, collective revenge, coercive interrogation, child execution training, trophy wealth, polygynous power accumulation, or sacred objects becoming leader cults. Center de-escalation where it appears, and expose the slide from deliverance into retaliation, wealth, and quasi-royal status.
